_comment: Off-viewport elements with TRUE negative origins, exactly as a web
are i32, so this snapshot deserializes with its real coordinates.
Before the origin was widened to i32, producers had to clamp x/y to 0.
That collapsed every off-screen node onto the viewport origin, where it
then fabricated `overlap` findings against whatever genuinely lives at
the top-left — the exact regression this fixture pins.
`skip-link` and `sr-only` are `left:-9999px` a11y nodes; `drawer` is an
off-canvas panel; `sticky-header` is scrolled half off the top so it
straddles the origin and DOES really overlap `page-title`.
